<!-- GENERATED by flowbook.tools.prompt.render_tool_catalog_doc(); do not edit by hand. Regenerate when REGISTRY changes. -->
# FlowBook tool catalog

The reproducibility tools every LLM surface (MCP, NBI, in-product fix-it) shares, defined once in `flowbook/tools/registry.py`.

- `alpha_rename(cell_id, old_name, new_name)` — Rename a variable from a cell onward (AST-based). Fixes variable reuse across cells: renames every occurrence in the target cell and all later code cells.
- `remove_inplace(cell_id, variable)` — Convert df.method(inplace=True) into df = df.method(). Fixes UNRECOVERABLE_MUTATION from pandas in-place operations.
- `insert_deepcopy(cell_id, variable)` — Insert copy.deepcopy() of a variable at the top of a cell and rename it downstream, breaking an aliasing/backward-mutation chain.
- `mark_diagnostic(cell_id)` — Prepend %diagnostic so a pure-inspection cell (df.info(), plots) is excluded from reproducibility tracking.
- `merge_cells(cell_ids)` — Merge consecutive code cells into the first, removing the rest. Use to keep a definition and its mutation in one atomic cell.
- `move_cell(cell_id, after_cell_id)` — Reorder a cell to directly after another (no source change). Use to move a diagnostic below the mutation it inspects.
